records.ety: [OE. bileafe, bileve; cf. AS. gelefa. See Believe.]

defs: Assent to a proposition or affirmation, or the acceptance of a fact, opinion, or assertion as real or true, without immediate personal knowledge; reliance upon word or testimony; partial or full assurance without positive knowledge or absolute certainty; persuasion; conviction; confidence; as, belief of a witness; the belief of our senses.
quotes: Belief admits of all degrees, from the slightest suspicion to the fullest assurance.

defs: A persuasion of the truths of religion; faith.
quotes: No man can attain [to] belief by the bare contemplation of heaven and earth.

defs: The thing believed; the object of belief.
quotes: Superstitious prophecies are not only the belief of fools, but the talk sometimes of wise men.

defs: A tenet, or the body of tenets, held by the advocates of any class of views; doctrine; creed.
quotes: In the heat of persecution to which Christian belief was subject upon its first promulgation.
